_meta.purpose: Recall@5 evaluation fixture for C3 visual-similarity (NFR ≥ 0.85, spec.md §5).
_meta.methodology: Crop-robustness recall. Each catalog row is the FULL public-domain image encoded once via the real SigLIP ONNX encoder (siglip2-base-patch16-224, pooler_output, L2-normalised). Each QUERY simulates a user photographing the artwork by center-cropping the SAME source image (sharp extract, fixed fraction) and encoding the crop. A query vector is therefore NEVER byte-identical to its stored row — it feeds different pixels through the same resize(224,224,fit:'fill') encoder, so query↔stored cosine is strictly < 1.0 (no trivial self-match). Recall@5 = fraction of queries whose source QID appears in findNearest(top-5) against the other catalog artworks (hardest realistic distractors) plus seeded random distractors.
_meta.measured: On this exact 4-image set with the real model present, recall@1 = recall@5 = 1.00 at every crop fraction 0.3–0.9. Self-cosine (crop vs full of same artwork) measured 0.585–0.991 (< 1.0 at every fraction → no self-match). Hardest cross-artwork distractor cosine measured 0.480–0.693; even a 30%-crop self-cosine (0.585) beats it, so this is a real recall test, not a round-trip. The asserted floor is 0.85 (0.15 margin), justified by measurement, not aspiration.
_meta.opsBacklog: The full 50-pair held-out benchmark remains a tracked ops item (NOT shipped). The shipped suite is crop-robustness recall over these 4 committed PD pairs; it is a real (non-vacuous) retrieval test, not the 50-pair benchmark. Each added pair MUST ship a committed public-domain image on disk under tests/fixtures/recall-images/ — never a path without a present PD file. Copyrighted works (e.g. Dalí d.1989, Picasso d.1973) are NOT eligible.
_meta.ciLane: .github/workflows/recall-e2e.yml — nightly cron (04:40 UTC) + push-to-main on encoder/repo/fixture/test paths + workflow_dispatch. Pulls the real ~355 MB SigLIP ONNX from the pinned GHCR base image via scripts/pull-siglip-model.sh, then runs RUN_INTEGRATION=true jest --testPathPattern=visual-similarity/recall against the createIntegrationHarness pgvector/pgvector:pg16 testcontainer. NON-blocking (not a branch-protection required check — recall is a quality signal, not a release gate) but REPORTED: the recall-alert job opens/closes a tracking GitHub issue (label nightly-recall-alert) so a red unattended run surfaces, mirroring ci-cd-mobile.yml maestro-full-alert.

pairs.title: Mona Lisa
pairs.provenance: Leonardo da Vinci, d.1519 — public domain; Wikimedia Commons. Committed copy of tests/ai/fixtures/art-mona-lisa.jpg.

pairs.title: Venus de Milo
pairs.provenance: Aphrodite of Milos, ancient Greek marble (c.150–125 BC) — public domain; Wikimedia Commons. Committed copy of tests/ai/fixtures/art-sculpture-venus.jpg.

pairs.title: Pont de Pierre, Bordeaux
pairs.provenance: Pont de Pierre, Bordeaux (1810–1822) — public-domain photograph; Wikimedia Commons. Committed copy of tests/ai/fixtures/building-monument.jpg.

pairs.title: Albert Einstein
pairs.provenance: Albert Einstein, PD-US photograph (1947) — public domain; Wikimedia Commons. Committed copy of tests/ai/fixtures/person-portrait.jpg. Included as a non-artwork-photo control to keep the catalog diverse.
